scope: Analysis only. No runtime code, deployment, merge, credentials, or production state were modified.
canonical_invariant: If no valid object exists -> nothing happens.

comparison_basis.head_commit_analyzed: 88acace Add runtime governance artifacts and reconcile execution/bypass maps
comparison_basis.head_targeted_result: 8 failing runtime/FATE assertions remained after PR #254 artifacts
comparison_basis.parent_targeted_result: the same 8 runtime/FATE assertions failed before PR #254
comparison_basis.classification_rule: If the same assertion failed on f2b9929 and 88acace, classify as PRE_EXISTING rather than RELATED_TO_PR or NEW_REGRESSION. Mark MASKED_DRIFT only as a drift signal, not as the primary regression source.

summary.merge_gate_recommendation: Do not merge runtime enforcement expansion until high-severity proof-lineage and exact-object drift is remediated or the tests are intentionally realigned with a reviewed runtime contract. PR #254 artifact changes did not introduce these failures.

failing_assertions.failing_test_name: authority lifecycle reserves before execution and consumes after execution
failing_assertions.affected_invariant: Used authority or reused execution objects must be blocked; every execution must produce proof before authority is finally consumed.
failing_assertions.replay_guarantees_weakened: Potentially unclear, but not proven by this assertion. Runtime still rejects authority reuse unless status is RESERVED or VALIDATED; drift is that proof consumes only status EXECUTED while the test expects status IN ('RESERVED','VALIDATED').
failing_assertions.observed_drift: Runtime uses an EXECUTED intermediate authority state before proof consumption, while the FATE assertion expects proof-time consumption from RESERVED or VALIDATED.
failing_assertions.recommended_remediation: Decide the canonical authority state machine. Either update runtime to consume from the reviewed expected states or update the FATE contract to explicitly permit RESERVED -> EXECUTED -> CONSUMED with proof-only consumption guards.

failing_assertions.failing_test_name: execution and proof preserve continuity lineage
failing_assertions.runtime_surface: src/index.ts /execute execution_registry insert and /proof proof_registry insert
failing_assertions.affected_invariant: session_id, identity_id, continuity_id, and continuity_hash must bind authority, validation, execution, and proof.
failing_assertions.exact_object_discipline_impacted: Indirectly. The same validated hash is present, but proof lineage construction is not represented in the explicit form asserted by FATE.
failing_assertions.observed_drift: Execution persists status EXECUTED and proof inserts lineage through INSERT ... SELECT with JSON strings containing runtime-bound continuity rather than the exact continuity-lineage contract asserted by the test.
failing_assertions.recommended_remediation: Remediate proof/execution lineage in a runtime-only follow-up: persist continuity_id and continuity_hash from actual authority/execution records, and make proof lineage evidence explicit and testable.

failing_assertions.failing_test_name: execution requires validated object hash to match the validated registry record
failing_assertions.runtime_surface: src/index.ts /execute validation_registry lookup and hash-mismatch telemetry
failing_assertions.affected_invariant: validated_object == executed_object, and invalid or missing validation must return NULL/blocked execution.
failing_assertions.observed_drift: Runtime does look up validation by decision_id, validated_object_hash, and invocation_nonce, but missing validation returns no_validation/execution_drift rather than the asserted hash_mismatch/HASH_MISMATCH signal.
failing_assertions.recommended_remediation: Normalize exact-object rejection semantics: distinguish missing validation from mutated hash only if both are intentional, then align reason codes and telemetry with that contract.

failing_assertions.failing_test_name: execution and proof preserve exact-object hash continuity
failing_assertions.runtime_surface: src/index.ts /execute execution_registry insert and /proof execution lookup/proof insert
failing_assertions.affected_invariant: The validated_object_hash accepted by /validate must be the same hash persisted by /execute and /proof.
failing_assertions.continuity_lineage_weakened: Indirectly, because proof insertion is lineage-sensitive and uses INSERT ... SELECT rather than explicit execution lineage variables.
failing_assertions.observed_drift: Runtime persists the hash, but the execution insert uses session_id and continuity_id ordering/status different from the asserted contract, and proof persistence is performed through INSERT ... SELECT rather than the explicit bind shape asserted by FATE.
failing_assertions.recommended_remediation: Review whether the current SQL shape is canonical. If yes, update FATE assertions to verify behavior/fields rather than exact source formatting; if no, refactor runtime proof/execution persistence in a bounded follow-up.

failing_assertions.failing_test_name: mutation after validation is rejected as NULL
failing_assertions.affected_invariant: Any mutation after validation must fail closed before execution.
failing_assertions.observed_drift: Runtime re-derives the compiled AEO hash and rejects mismatch, but uses reason wrong_hash rather than the asserted hash_mismatch reason. The hash_drift telemetry class is present.
failing_assertions.recommended_remediation: Unify exact-object mismatch reason naming across validate/execute/proof so post-validation mutation has one canonical NULL reason and telemetry indicator.

failing_assertions.failing_test_name: runtime contains exact-object mismatch failure signals
failing_assertions.runtime_surface: src/index.ts /validate scope/workflow constraints and /execute hash mismatch signals
failing_assertions.affected_invariant: Runtime must expose deterministic exact-object mismatch signals for policy, test, and audit consumers.
failing_assertions.exact_object_discipline_impacted: Terminology drift only; runtime uses scope_constraints_mismatch rather than the asserted scope_mismatch signal.
failing_assertions.observed_drift: The expected signal scope_mismatch is absent; runtime uses scope_constraints_mismatch and workflow_mismatch.
failing_assertions.recommended_remediation: Standardize exact-object failure vocabulary in policy/tests. Prefer one canonical reason set and map legacy aliases only in diagnostics if needed.

failing_assertions.failing_test_name: proof creation requires matching execution lineage
failing_assertions.runtime_surface: src/index.ts /proof execution_registry lookup and orphaned proof rejection
failing_assertions.affected_invariant: Every proof must be derived from an actual execution with matching execution_id, decision_id, and validated_object_hash.
failing_assertions.continuity_lineage_weakened: Potentially, if proof diagnostics cannot distinguish orphaned execution from lineage mismatch.
failing_assertions.observed_drift: Runtime loads execution by execution_id, decision_id, and validated_object_hash, but returns execution_missing/proof_without_execute rather than missing_execution/orphaned_proof_attempt.
failing_assertions.recommended_remediation: Normalize proof rejection reasons and telemetry indicators, then add behavior-level tests for orphaned proof, mismatched hash, and mismatched session lineage.

failing_assertions.failing_test_name: proof creation binds authority and execution lineage into persisted proof
failing_assertions.runtime_surface: src/index.ts /proof proof_registry INSERT and authority consumption transaction
failing_assertions.affected_invariant: Every proof must persist authority lineage and execution lineage that match the execution boundary object.
failing_assertions.observed_drift: Runtime persists authority_lineage and execution_lineage columns, but constructs them inline as JSON.stringify payloads with runtime-bound continuity rather than explicit authorityLineage/executionLineage evidence that includes authority_id, identity_id, actual continuity_id, and validated hash lineage.
failing_assertions.recommended_remediation: In a dedicated runtime patch, construct proof lineage from loaded authority/execution/continuity records, compare authority_lineage and execution_lineage before insert, and persist exact lineage evidence atomically with proof.

global_findings: All 8 assertions are PRE_EXISTING relative to PR #254 because the same targeted runtime/FATE tests failed at parent commit f2b9929 and at head commit 88acace.
No failing assertion is RELATED_TO_PR or a NEW_REGRESSION from the governance artifact changes.
The failures cluster around runtime contract drift in authority lifecycle status names, exact-object mismatch reason naming, and proof/continuity lineage evidence shape.
Non-bypassability is not directly weakened by these assertions because no direct deploy, direct DB mutation, or alternate execution route is introduced by PR #254.
Exact-object discipline and proof/continuity lineage require follow-up runtime remediation before declaring the GitHub-governed production deploy surface fully locked.
commands_used: git worktree add --detach /tmp/mindshift-parent f2b9929 && cd /tmp/mindshift-parent && node --test tests/fate/authority-lifecycle-consumption.test.mjs tests/fate/continuity-lineage-enforcement.test.mjs tests/fate/exact-object-enforcement.test.mjs tests/fate/exact-object-rederivation.test.mjs tests/fate/proof-lineage-enforcement.test.mjs
